name: Skylar Detunya | Rogue Warp Survivor
description:
As fire hissed from the broken fuel lines, the Captain rose from her purported tomb…

“I survived? I’m the only one who survived?”

Short 3D animation by Wanoco4D to summarize the crash

Main Character Storyline (If you’re interested in the full story! If not, skip down to the end!)

After a surprise attack carried out by the Ueclian Attack Coalition against the Sedeva Defense Force (Both advanced alien races), a massive show of power and presence shocked the solar system’s vast population. A new weapon, more specifically a new Hypercarrier (a space super-carrier on steroids) known as the Ueclian “Ojira” was revealed to be the leading front against nearly unstoppable assaults. Three kilometers of advanced alloys, machinery, weapons, and top-of-the-line tech all across the board. Without proper reinforcements at the ready, the SDF didn’t stand a chance. In just twenty hours, the SDF lost twelve vital supply and fuel depots, manufacturing facilities, and military compounds to the seemingly indestructible monster of a machine.

But every monster has a weakness, and sometimes those weaknesses could be calculated. The size of the Ojira was astronomical, and therefore it used a colossal amount of fuel. The SDF estimated the amount, then calculated which known UAC fuel depots could deliver as much, and found only one choice. From there, a collective engagement of several SDF space carriers and destroyers was set in place…:

Just outside of Perimeter Zone 3, UAC controlled mass fuel depot

Skylar and her team were waiting in their “Jackal” Multi-role spacecrafts on the deck of the SDF “QuaDCAT” supercarrier. After hours of quadruple checking everything, a green-lit go ahead was sent out, and in jumped four of the most powerful warships the SDF had available. This was a hail Mary, and despite the uneven numbers, this was quite the fair fight. From the moment the SDF warships stabilized from their jump, missiles, torpedos, ballistic cannons, energy weapons, everything was set to full force. UAC turrets surrounding the refueling depot were destroyed very quickly, which cued the QuaDCAT to join the fight.

The jump rattled the ship, but after a few seconds of stabilizing, the “Jackals” were rising out of the launch bays and shooting out of the carrier deck like a swarm of wasps. Giant cannon fire, anti-air emplacements, plasmatic charges, all phased through space like a desperate test of skill and willpower. Those who hesitated, lacked the skill, or were just straight unlucky were turned into a ball of flame faster than one could register. But luckily, Cpt. Detunya’s team was among the best. With skills at the top and hesitation below zero, they were ready to face every threat that they could imagine, and this one above all, the one to save their species.

The team weaved through the predicted fire paths, and with a splash of luck, the six fighters made it to the hull of the Ojira. They maglocked to the frame, the metal ‘kechunk’ of the landing locks completely inaudible by the rapid firing of countless cannons shaking the Ojira, and then made the most dangerous EVA in history to the nearest access door. The thick maintenance door cracked open with a few seconds of expert hacking, and in went the infiltration team into the mobile super weapon.

“Planned route is being sent to our HUDs, keep as quiet as you can but move as fast as ligh
